title: Users with the highest total order amount
description: Return the user_id and total_amount for the user(s) with the highest total order amount. If multiple users tie, include all. Columns: user_id, total_amount. Order by user_id ascending.
expected_query: SELECT user_id, total_amount FROM (SELECT u.user_id, SUM(o.amount) AS total_amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id) t WHERE total_amount = (SELECT MAX(total_amount) FROM (SELECT SUM(o.amount) AS total_amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id)) ORDER BY user_id ASC
solution_query: SELECT user_id, total_amount FROM (SELECT u.user_id, SUM(o.amount) AS total_amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id) t WHERE total_amount = (SELECT MAX(total_amount) FROM (SELECT SUM(o.amount) AS total_amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id)) ORDER BY user_id ASC;
explanation: Aggregate order amounts per user, find the maximum, and return all users matching that maximum.
hints: Return all users matching the maximum.
